purpose: Adds a check in hci_cmd_sync_queue to return an error when the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set, preventing queuing of commands after a device has been unregistered and avoiding a potential use‑after‑free.
Removes an unnecessary asm/termios.h include from n_hdlc.c, relying on linux/tty.h to provide termios definitions, thereby cleaning up header dependencies.
Updates documentation to include the new 'N/A' store metric and adjust report table references.
Fixes race and double‑free bugs in hugetlb vma lock handling during vma split and unmapping, and adds defensive checks to prevent future lock‑related bugs.
Adds AVDD and MICVDD supply definitions to the Realtek rt5682s device tree binding, documenting two power supplies used by the codec.
Adds missing iounmap() in zorro7xx_remove_one() to free memory mapped by ioremap() when probe fails, eliminating a resource leak.
This commit removes an unused 'work' field from the mlx5e_async_ctx structure in the mlx5e kTLS code, cleaning up the data structure and eliminating dead code.
Changes cfg80211_auth_request::key_idx from unsigned to signed to allow -1 value, avoiding static checker warnings when key is NULL and key_idx unused.
Reorders the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS entries in the RISC‑V defconfig to match savedefconfig ordering, purely cosmetic.
Adds a helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to replace inline checks for CAN‑FD mode configuration, improving code clarity and maintainability.
Adds logic to the RTC selftests to skip the test when no RTC hardware is present, preventing false failures.
Removes a redundant assignment to the variable 'bytes' in echoaudio midi.c, eliminating a dead store warning and cleaning up code.

This commit changes the kernel build configuration logic so that enabling rcuscale no longer forces the inclusion of RCU Tasks when CONFIG_PREEMPT_NONE=y. It removes an unnecessary dependency, reducing kernel size and simplifying tracing.
This patch removes a duplicated 'be split' string in the KVM x86 MMU header spte.h, cleaning up redundant text.

Reverts a change that populated the dcn315 clock table based on dcfclk, which caused underflow when dcfclk states are disabled. The revert prevents a potential bug until a better solution is implemented.
Adds a compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Mali-G31 GPU in the device tree bindings.
Renames the struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp in the dw PCIe core driver to align naming with other dw_ prefixed structures, improving consistency and readability.
Changes the format specifier in netdev_dbg from %#06hx to %#06x to match the promoted int argument, eliminating a clang warning and preventing potential undefined behavior.
Fixes a signed comparison bug in MIPS jump_label branch range check by casting upper bound to long, preventing negative offset warnings.
The commit removes the HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD case from SetHwReg8188EU(), calling the underlying function directly to eliminate an unnecessary HAL layer wrapper.
Adds static keyword to non-global symbols in arm64 errata code to silence sparse warnings and limit scope.
Removes an incorrect 'interrupt-flags' cell from MDSS device tree bindings, correcting the provider-consumer relationship and preventing misconfiguration.
Adds dynamic allocation for arrays in struct ctrl_ref, enabling drivers to specify array sizes at runtime, preparing for future flexibility.
Adds support for additional Qualcomm SoCs and PMICs to the socinfo driver, expanding device recognition for SM8350, SC8280XP, SA8540P, SM8450, and related PMICs.
Adds support for all chip cuts A–P in the rtl8xxxu Wi‑Fi driver, replacing the previous 'unknown' output for cuts greater than E with the correct letter.
Removes the custom .is_enabled flag from PRCMU clock structures, relying on the core's existing enable count. Simplifies clock state handling and eliminates redundant bookkeeping.
Reorganizes XIP_KERNEL conditional macros in RISC‑V memory init, grouping related definitions and moving pt_ops macro to reduce #ifdef usage, improving code clarity and maintainability.
Adds device tree entries to enable QUP and GPI DMA on the SM6350-Lena platform, allowing future I2C nodes to use DMA.
This patch updates comments in the rzg2l thermal driver, corrects a typo, and enforces coding style by moving comment delimiters to separate lines.
Adds a check for HW semaphore acquisition in release_swfw_sync; if acquisition fails (e.g., hardware surprise removal), logs an error and exits the loop to avoid an infinite loop.
Updates the pcm3168a ASoC driver to replace legacy bus clock defines with modern DAI format terminology, removing old style defines and aligning the driver with current ASoC clocking conventions.
Removes the IS_SWAPFILE preprocessor check from NFS code, as the macro is always defined, eliminating unnecessary code and potential confusion.
Adds GTP echo response handling to the kernel GTP device, sending GTP_ECHO_RSP with recovery IE and restart counter when an echo request is received, ensuring compliance with GTP spec.
Replaces cpumask_next_* calls with cpumask_first_* where appropriate, adds cpumask_first_zero() helper, improving correctness and performance.
The commit removes an unsupported firmware call (dtim_assoc IOVAR) from the brcmfmac driver, eliminating repeated error messages after AP roam.
Adds a new board configuration (adl_rt5682) for Intel's ASoC driver, enabling the alc5682 headset codec on platforms lacking a speaker amplifier by connecting it to SSP0.
Adds a check in io_uring net sendmsg path to return error when zero-copy sendmsg is requested on sockets that don't support it, mirroring behavior for send requests.
Adds a check for xeCJK.sty in the kernel documentation preamble, preventing errors when the package is missing or broken.
The commit updates the checkpatch script to suppress false error messages for macros that contain the keyword "case", which are common in kernel drivers.
Replace simple-KMS atomic helpers with regular atomic helpers, remove empty helpers, add unconditional plane state check; no functional changes.
Adds a JPEG decoding worker interface to the MediaTek JPEG decoder driver, enabling three hardware decoders to run concurrently on MT8195.
Removes redundant VPRINTK() debug prints and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() helper from libata, cleaning up code.

Adds logic to enable AMD display's zstate during extended vblank when entering FREESYNC_STATE_VIDEO, calculating extra vblank time, and setting min_dst_y_next_start register; restores defaults on exit.
Changes the hqd_destroy() callback prototype to use uint32_t for reset_type instead of enum kfd_preempt_type, fixing a CFI failure on Clang kernels.
Corrects the STM32 pinctrl binding property name from 'reset' to 'resets' and adds 'additionalProperties: false' to enforce schema, fixing an existing bug and preventing future ones.
Adds a compiler directive to silence GCC 12 array-bounds warning in ath9k driver, preventing noisy warnings during compilation.
Replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in sun8i-ss crypto driver to simplify code and use a more appropriate runtime PM API.

The commit moves the fixed file installation and removal logic from the io_uring request handling code into the filetable subsystem, and changes helper functions to accept a context instead of an io_kiocb. This reorganizes code for better modularity and clarity.
Adds separate G1 and G2 decoder nodes in the i.MX8MQ VPU device tree bindings, enabling independent power-domain control via the vpu-blk-ctrl driver.
The commit removes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro, which was only used once inside IWL_DEVICE_BZ, and inlines its contents directly into that macro, simplifying the code.
